title: rubus tanakae var. glaber
intro: Rubus tanakae var. glaber, also known as the Tanakae raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus tanakae plant, a type of wild raspberry native to Japan. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus tanakae var. glaber include potential relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
